ops.title: golangci-lint's GitHub Actions cache can emit phantom staticcheck findings on untouched files; delete the cache entry and rerun instead of touching code
ops.body:
cc-notes CI's golangci-lint-action cache produced phantom SA5011 nil-deref findings in `internal/viz/api_test.go` (2026-07-07) — code guarded by `t.Fatalf`, unchanged for weeks, same golangci version green hours earlier. Trigger: a push that changed the flagged package's sibling/dependency packages while the action restored a stale analysis cache.

**Why:** the restored cache carries stale staticcheck facts; a clean-cache local run of the identical version reported 0 issues, proving the finding was cache rot, not code.

**How to apply:** when CI lint flags code the diff didn't touch, first reproduce locally with `golangci-lint cache clean && golangci-lint run`. If clean, delete the poisoned entry (`gh cache list -R <repo> | grep golangci`, then `gh cache delete <id> -R <repo>`) and `gh run rerun <id> --failed`. Never "fix" the untouched code or add nolint to satisfy a phantom ([[dont-assert-absence-without-a-thorough-search]] energy: verify the tool, not just the code).
